response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile senior engineer with strong systems and performance instincts, but the profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments a decade ago, and his recent decade centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang services rather than C++/STL application architecture. He shows no exposure to MQTT, industrial automation protocols, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, which are core rather than advantageous for this position. An eight-month unexplained employment gap in 2020 is a minor note relative to the skills mismatch.
strengths: Deep low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby, handling millions of requests per second
Long track record of mentoring and knowledge-sharing (workshops, dojos, courses), matching the role's mentorship expectations
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testable design, and open-source project ownership
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, ended 2015); C++ is not even listed in his skills section
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, IEC 62443, or secure-development-lifecycle background, all core to this role
Career is oriented toward web/full-stack product work, with an unexplained ~8-month gap between January and September 2020

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 and MQTT experience
